title: Optimising Renders (memo, useCallback, useMemo)
description: Prevent unnecessary re‑renders with React.memo and hooks.

slides.title: When Do Components Re‑render?
slides.content:
# Re‑render Triggers

A component re‑renders when:
- Its state changes
- Its parent re‑renders (by default, all children re‑render)
- Context value changes

Often, children re‑render even if their props haven't changed. This is wasted work.

slides.title: React.memo
useCallback and useMemo

quizData.question: What is the purpose of `React.memo`?

options.text: To prevent a component from re‑rendering if props haven't changed

quizData.explanation: `React.memo` memoises the component output based on props.

slides.title: Pro Tip 🚀
slides.content: Don't over‑optimise. Start with clean code, then profile to find actual bottlenecks before adding memoisation.
